text.title: Agency Valuation Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of Agency Valuation Rubric enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every dimension in the Agency Valuation Rubric scorecard has anchors written for the 1, 3, and 5 levels — minimum. No anchors → no scoring.
rule.rationale: Unanchored scales drift between raters. '4 out of 5' means nothing without a behavioral anchor.

rule.statement: Each score must cite the specific evidence used (quote, log line, doc paragraph). A score without evidence is a guess.
rule.rationale: Evidence forces the rater to ground the score. Unevidenced scores fail audit and inter-rater reliability.

rule.statement: For decisions ≥$10k or strategic, require ≥2 independent raters and reconciliation if scores diverge by >1 point on any dimension.

rationale.source: delphi method / Linstone & Turoff, Delphi Method (1975)

rule.rationale: Single-rater scores are dominated by mood, recency, and halo. Reconciliation forces explicit talk-through.

rule.statement: Final composite is a weighted blend with weights fixed BEFORE scoring (otherwise raters reverse-engineer to the outcome).
rule.rationale: Post-hoc weight tuning is rubric-laundering. Pre-registration of weights protects against motivated reasoning.

example.title: Anchored dimension in a discovery-call scorecard
example.code: Budget fit (1-5): 1 = no budget mentioned 3 = budget within 30% of typical engagement 5 = budget at or above typical engagement, signoff confirmed
example.note: Anchors at 1/3/5 give raters concrete behaviors to score against.

example.title: Unanchored dimension
example.code: Budget fit (1-5): rate from low to high
example.note: Two raters will score the same call 2 vs 4. Decisions diverge.
